headline: Oil Prices Rise, S&P 500 Futures Fall As Reserve Plans Offset Mine Fears
summary: Oil prices remained in a holding pattern early Wednesday as plans for a joint release of emergency petroleum reserves moved forward offset fears that the Strait of Hormuz could remain closed indefinitely amid concerns that Iranian vessels are laying mines.  S&P 500 futures rose slightly, helped by a strong earnings report from Oracle.  The International Energy Agency has recommended the release of a record 400 million barrels of oil by its 32-member nations, according to The Wall Street Journal.

headline: How Strait of Hormuz closure can become tipping point for global economy
summary: A Strait of Hormuz closure due to U.S.-Iran war hit the oil market hard, but sectors reliant on shipping across economy from metals to farming are vulnerable.

headline: Here's the inflation breakdown for February 2026 — in one chart
summary: The consumer price index was rose 2.4% in February 2026, above policymakers' target, economists said. War in Iran complicates the picture.

headline: EXCLUSIVE: Fertilizer Stocks Are the Market's Hottest Trade. The War In Iran Explains Why.
summary: Investors have piled into fertilizer stocks since U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran. The Strait of Hormuz — through which a third of global fertilizer trade flows — tells you why.
